title: Fastify server + MVP REST + WebSocket
acceptance_criteria: src/api/server.ts exports buildServer({ db: Pool, redis: Redis }): FastifyInstance — creates a Fastify instance with logger enabled; registers @fastify/cors (allow all origins in dev — CORS_ORIGIN env var); registers @fastify/websocket; registers dashboard and paper-trades route plugins; returns the instance without starting it (start is done by caller)

src/api/routes/dashboard.ts: GET /dashboard/live — queries straddle_snapshots for the latest row WHERE time > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 minute' (always time-filtered); returns { straddleValue, roc, acceleration, atmStrike, underlying, timestamp }; GET /dashboard/summary — queries paper_trades WHERE DATE(entry_time AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata') = today; returns array of { id, status, entryStraddleValue, currentPnl, exitReason }

src/api/routes/paper-trades.ts: GET /paper-trades — accepts query params ?date (YYYY-MM-DD, defaults to today IST) and ?status ('open'|'closed'|'all'); queries paper_trades with date filter always using AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata'; returns paginated array of PaperTrade rows

src/api/websocket.ts: WS /ws/ticks — subscribes to STREAM_STRADDLE via Redis XREAD (not XREADGROUP — this is a broadcast, not a consumer group); broadcasts each straddle snapshot as JSON to all connected WebSocket clients; handles client disconnect gracefully; logs WARN if no clients connected (not an error)
All routes use Fastify JSON Schema validation for both request params and response shape
Graceful shutdown: server.close() drains existing connections; exported closeServer() unsubscribes from Redis and closes the Fastify server
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
